details: Quote, Chart, Search, Screener, QuoteSummary, Options, and future observed endpoints.
boundary: Document observed behavior; do not claim official support unless cited from official sources.

details: Track field names, JSON paths, data types, applicability, validation rules, and CSV/database guidance.
boundary: Field existence may vary by symbol, security type, market state, and date.

details: Record new, removed, renamed, moved, null-only, and type-changing fields.
boundary: Require capture evidence and a status field.

details: Maintain stock, ETF, closed-end fund, mutual fund, futures, index, crypto, and forex test symbols.
boundary: Symbol set is representative, not exhaustive.

details: Track observed marketState values and session-dependent field behavior.
boundary: Do not assume all values are complete until observed and retested.

details: Document what a public user can observe through an app UI or exported files.
boundary: Do not describe these as source-code fixes unless the developer confirms implementation details.

details: Provide CSV templates for run logs, result interpretation, and change records.
boundary: Templates should be easy for non-programmers to fill in.

details: Scripts may check table structure, IDs, required columns, and classification consistency.
boundary: Scripts are support tools, not the main purpose of the repository.

details: The project may contain prices, symbols, and fields, but does not recommend securities.
Do not provide methods to evade rate limits, anti-bot controls, authentication, or terms of service.
boundary: Document public observation and failures such as HTTP 429 without evasion instructions.

details: This project is not a data vendor or service-level agreement.
boundary: Observed delays, nulls, and failures should be documented as observed behavior.

details: Do not include instructions to scrape authenticated, private, or paid content.
boundary: Keep public captures and user-provided app exports separate from restricted sources.
